info.title: Engaging Networks Services REST API
info.description:
This document describes the endpoints currently available as part of the 'Engaging Networks Services' (ENS) API. 


## Intended audience ##
These services are intended for use by integration partners who are familiar with the Engaging Networks platform, and who require a secure interface to handle data held within the platform.  The services can also be used to conduct basic processing of campaign pages to utilise some of the functionality provided by the platform .


## Using this document ##
The documentation is provided in an interactive format, and requires a modern web-browser to work correctly.  The dynamic nature of the documentation means that you are able to input example parameters which may specifically relate to your use case, such as authentication details or parameters required to perform a call.  The calls are not meant to be run directly from the browser as certain restrictions may be in place to prevent this, but the examples and generated code can be easily copied into a text editor for further processing. 


For these services, the URL used to access them depends on where the client account is located. As a client's account may be located on servers either in the United States or Canada,  they are distingushed by the use of a 'base url', which can be selected when formulating a request. A summary of the 'API Base URL' defined can be found at the top of this overview.


## API Usage notes ##
<!-- theme: info --> 
> #### API call limits
> There is a limit of **5,000** requests per **1 hour** per API User. For page processing, there is a limit of maximum **200** requests per **5 minutes** from a **single source IP address**. *Beyond these limits, requests will be blocked.*
**To get started using these definitions with your favourite development environment, request an 'export' above and simply import the resulting file into a tool of choice.**

Authenticates an API User and returns a session token, which is required for calling on the services. 

All API Users are managed by an account administrator, who must ensure that the users are assigned the appropriate permissions, and that all IP addresses to be used with the services have been whitelisted as part of the user setup.

post.description:
Process a page submission to an existing Engaging Networks page.


Note: Payloads that include live credit card details will require the use of a [separate endpoint](/operations/processCardPayment).** 
Your code should be able to determine which endpoint is appropriate to use based on whether 'ccnumber' and/or 'ccvv' is part of the transaction. 
### Supported page types ###
The API currently supports processing of a number of the pagetypes available on the platform. Note that the type is set when the page is created in the dashboard, and can not be altered. 


All page processing requires a *'supporter'* object to be submitted (which can include a *'questions'* object containing the corresponding responses).


**dcf** - A generic pagetype used to capture data. 


**pet** - Petition page.


**ems** - Typically used as a sign-up page. 

 
**unsub** - A subscribe/unsubscribe page, or subscription management page.


**ev** - Event page. Requires an *'event'* object in the body, which includes the *'tickets'* and the *'attendees'* for each ticket. The page also requires a *'transaction'* object to be submitted, to provide payment details for the tickets purchased.


Providing a list of attendees indicates the number of attendee tickets being purchased, so if you pass in one attendee you are buying a single ticket, if you pass in 5 you are buying this quantity of tickets.


If an event ticket is set up to be supplied at no cost to the supporter, 'cash' will be used as the **paymenttype** instead of any value provided for this field within in the transaction object - unless an additional donation amount or other details are provided. 
 To supply an additional donation to the order, you can use **additonalAmount** in the *event* object. This amount will then be added to the total charged.
 The transaction element for an event does not require a **donationAmt** to be passed, as this will be calculated based on the number of tickets purchased.


**mem** - Membership. Requires a *'membership'* object, which should include an array of 'members'.
 If a free membership is required, it is possible to provide a 'donationAmt' with a value of 0. If a free membership is selected, 'cash' will be used to as the **paymenttype** instead of any value provided for this field within the transaction object

**et** - Email to Target page. Requires a *'contactMessage'* object containing the *'subject'* and *'message'*. 


**nd** - Donation pages **without** a *'subType'* are supported, however Premium donations can also be processed.  Requires a *'transaction'* object containing the payment details. For a Premium donation, an additional *'product'* object containing the selected item may also be included, if a selection is required. If shipping is required for any item selected as part of a Premium donation process, optional shipping details may also be added to the *'transaction'* object.


To determine whether the page you are trying to post into is supported, you can use [*/page/{id}*](/operations/getPageDetails) where you can view details about the **type** and **subType** of the page, as well as other settings.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The database identifier of a page.

requestBody.description: Description of supporter and page related data to be processed by this request

demo.description: Request the page process run in 'demo' mode, which for donation processing means using a 'test' gateway with appropriate testing credentials rather than real cards, and for advocacy actions such as email to target all email traffic generated will go to the participating supporter's email address.

suppressAutoResponder.description: Do not send an autoresponder ('thank you email') after the page submission is completed.

standardFieldNames.description: Use standardised rather than account specific names within the 'supporter' object when referring to fields.

trackingId.description: A value which is usually used to denote the origin of incoming traffic. May be passed as a the parameter as part of a link to a page using: **ea.tracking.id**

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

supporter.description:
Describes a supporter with the fields named using standardised field names. These can be utilised in [**/page/process**](pageProcess) to simplify code targetting supporter fields.

Note that the software allows for great flexibility when it comes to naming of fields, which means that the 'non-standard' names for each field will be dependent on the account.

emailAddress.title: supporter email
emailAddress.description: The primary email address of a supporter, which will typically be used as the basis for identifying a unique supporter to which any transactions will be associated.

title.title: supporter title
title.description: A supporter's title to be used in salutations

address1.title: supporter address 1
address1.description: The first line of the supporter's address

address2.title: supporter address 2
address2.description: The second line of the supporter's address

address3.title: supporter address 3
address3.description: The third line of the supporter's address

postcode.title: supporter postcode
postcode.description: The supporter's postal or zip code

city.title: supporter city
city.description: The city in a supporter's address

region.title: supporter region
region.description: The supporter's region, state, county, province or similar

phoneNumber.title: supporter phonenumber
phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number for a supporter

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

anonymousDonor.description: Flag to indicate if a donation is being made anonymously. The flag is stored on the supporter record, but can be passed with individual donations if the field is included on a form.  **Anonymous donors names should not be publicised.**

creditCardHolderName.title: card holder name
creditCardHolderName.description: The name of the credit card holder, as printed on the card. To be used when processing donations.

bankAccountNumber.title: bank account number
bankAccountNumber.description: The number of the bank account used for making donations.

bankRoutingNumber.title: routing number
bankRoutingNumber.description: A bank routing number - sometimes known as a 'sort code' - required for donations via bank.

bankAccountType.description: A gateway dependent value for the type of bank account to charge, e.g CHECKING or SAVINGS.

emailAddress2.description:
Supporter's secondary email address. 

 **Note:** While this email field does **not** count as an indicator of the uniqueness of record, system settings may affect how this field behaves in particular with relation to external CRM systems.
  Such a setting may be enabled if your organization represents a 'Contact' with multiple email addresses. Any email address used on submission will be also checked against this field to identify a supporter. 
  If a match is found then a new supporter record will *not* be created, and the transaction will be associated with this supporter.

secondAddress2.description: Supporter's secondary address - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

phoneNumber2.description: Supporter's secondary phone number, e.g cell phone or mobile number. This field is used by the Messaging services to handle delivery via SMS.

billingAddress2.description: Supporter's billing address - second line (floor, apartment, suite, etc. )

organization.title: supporter organization
organization.description: The name of the supporter's organization

chapter.description: The chapter of an organization this supporter belongs to

suffix.description: The suffix to use for the constituent

dateOfBirth.description: Supporter's date of birth. The format YYYY-MM-DD is **required** for this field by the software.

geo.description: The supporter's geographical location, as indicated by coordinates supplied as latitude and longitude.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location, North or South (-) of the equator.

NOT_TAGGED_1.description:
Up to 300 generic fields can be created to be used to store any type of information. 

 These fields are automatically assigned the next available number (1-300) when they are added as part of an *Account Data Structure*, so their functional assignment in a particular account should be determined by a call to **/supporter/fields**, or by a quick visit to the dashboard where both the field name and the assigned 'tag' can be observed.

 **DO NOT** push any data into numbered fields that have not yet been assigned a function in the account's *Data Structure*.

questions.description: An object containing the questions we wish to record answers to for this supporter.  In the dashboard questions are organised as 'Questions' and 'Opt-in'. While both types are technically questions, their function is slightly different.  'Questions' are quite generic in nature, have a user defined role and can be used to collect (almost) any type of information. Questions created as an 'Opt-in' type have a specific role in the software, as they are used for recording supporter consent relating to any type of communication.

Opt-in.description:
An example question with the dashboard name 'Opt-in', set up in the dashboard to be an Opt-in.

 When the *standardFieldNames* parameter is used in /page/process, it is also possible to refer to questions by their id (as seen in /supporter/questions) using a name such as *question.{id}*'

Comments.description: An **example** question with the dashboard name 'Comments', set up in the dashboard to be a generic type of Question
Comments.example: I decided to opt-out of receiving your newsletters because I have too much stuff to read...

post.summary: Process a Card Payment Page Request
post.description:
### Better security for direct processing of card payments ###
All credit card data submitted through this service will be tokenised before being processed. 

<!-- theme: info --> 
> #### When to use
> This endpoint is noted by the presence of the appendix '**-vault**' in the *host* part of the base URL, and should be used to process **live** card donations which have credit card details included in the transaction object (specifically '**ccnumber**' and '**ccvv**'). 
> #### Please note
> This service is only suitable for processing requests that do not require any third-party redirects or similar during page processing - such as lightboxes or redirects. This means that **payments such as PayPal (pay via PayPal), Venmo, Apple Pay, Google Pay and Chariot DAF are not supported**.  
The endpoint should not be used for processing where a 'demo' mode is being used for testing (e.g when using the gateway supplied 'test card' details), or where the payment does *not* include any credit card information (such as bank, check or cash type transactions), or for any other types of page processing, e.g advocacy or engagement activity etc.

 
For more examples of page processing, and for processing other types of page objects (including how to test flows using demo mode), please refer to [*/page/{id}/processPage*](/operations/processPage).

  If a match is found then a new supporter record will *not* be created, and the transaction will be associated with this supporter.

phoneNumber2.description: Supporter's secondary phone number, e.g cell phone or mobile number. This field is used by the Messaging services to handle delivery via SMS.

billingAddress2.description: Supporter's billing address - second line (floor, apartment, suite, etc. )

organization.title: supporter organization
organization.description: The name of the supporter's organization

chapter.description: The chapter of an organization this supporter belongs to

suffix.description: The suffix to use for the constituent

dateOfBirth.description: Supporter's date of birth. The format YYYY-MM-DD is **required** for this field by the software.

geo.description: The supporter's geographical location, as indicated by coordinates supplied as latitude and longitude.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location, North or South (-) of the equator.

longitude.description: The longitude of the location, East or West (-) of the Greenwhich meridian.

sendOffset.description: Nearest Time Offset of the supporter's location.  When updating manually, the value **must** be in the correct format, and be one of the supported offset times (positive or negative) in hours and minutes from UTC.  Offsets that appear outside of the allowed values are shifted to the nearest available supported timezone.

NOT_TAGGED_1.description:
Up to 300 generic fields can be created to be used to store any type of information. 

 These fields are automatically assigned the next available number (1-300) when they are added as part of an *Account Data Structure*, so their functional assignment in a particular account should be determined by a call to **/supporter/fields**, or by a quick visit to the dashboard where both the field name and the assigned 'tag' can be observed.

 **DO NOT** push any data into numbered fields that have not yet been assigned a function in the account's *Data Structure*.

questions.description: An object containing the questions we wish to record answers to for this supporter.  In the dashboard questions are organised as 'Questions' and 'Opt-in'. While both types are technically questions, their function is slightly different.  'Questions' are quite generic in nature, have a user defined role and can be used to collect (almost) any type of information. Questions created as an 'Opt-in' type have a specific role in the software, as they are used for recording supporter consent relating to any type of communication.

Opt-in.description:
An example question with the dashboard name 'Opt-in', set up in the dashboard to be an Opt-in.

 When the *standardFieldNames* parameter is used in /page/process, it is also possible to refer to questions by their id (as seen in /supporter/questions) using a name such as *question.{id}*'

Comments.description: An **example** question with the dashboard name 'Comments', set up in the dashboard to be a generic type of Question
Comments.example: I decided to opt-out of receiving your newsletters because I have too much stuff to read...

transaction.description: Provides the necessary parameters required to set up a netdonor payment process.

donationAmt.description: Processing amount, presented as a decimal number without any currency symbols.  The amount may be omitted for pagetypes where the amount to be processed is calculated by the page processing, for example when purchasing tickets for events or certain types of symbolic gifts. The amount will also include any additions made by any optional Fee Cover requested.

paymenttype.title: payment type
paymenttype.description: Passed as a value or determined by a payment gateway, this describes the payment type to use for the transaction. Valid types may vary depending on the payment gateway used.	  Some payment types, e.g **CASH**, **CHECK** or **WIRE** may be used to indicate offline donations, or to process 'free' items. Such donations will not be possible to reconcile with an external payment gateway.	 In output, the prefix **TEST:** indicates a transaction carried out in a *DEMO* mode. These transactions should also not be considered for reconciliation purposes. 	 The prefix **digitalwallet:** is added if the payment was made using a digital wallet integration.

paycurrency.title: currency
paycurrency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the currency used for this transaction. When processing payments, it is important to note that currency support is gateway dependent, so be sure to check with your designated payment gateway that the selected currency is supported and has been enabled for your account.

ccnumber.title: credit card number
ccnumber.description: The credit card number to charge when processing this payment. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

ccexpire.title: card expiry
ccexpire.description: The card expiry date. The exact format is dependent on the payment gateway used to process the transaction.

ccvv.description: A verification value or code printed on the credit card being used for this transaction. May be 3 or 4 characters depending on the card issuer. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

recurrpay.title: Recurring Payment
recurrpay.description: This donation should be recurring according to a schedule.

recurrfreq.title: Recurring Frequency
recurrfreq.description:
The schedule used for processing recurring payments. 

 Typical frequency for recurring charges are usually monthly, quarterly, every six months ('semi annually'), or annually.

recurrday.title: Recurring Day
recurrday.description: For recurring payment schedules, this denotes the day of the month when a payment should be processed.  If a Recurring Start Date was also provided, the processing day will be valid for the periods after the *initial* payment.

recurrstart.title: ISO formatted Recurring Start Date
recurrstart.description: The date when the *first* payment in a recurring payment schedule should be pushed [MM/DD/YYYY]. Works in conjunction with *Recurring Day*

recurrend.title: Recurring Expiry
recurrend.description: The expiry date of the card associated with this recurring payment [MM/YYYY].

recurrcnt.title: Recurring Count
recurrcnt.description: The number of recurring donation payments to process with this schedule.

recurprd.title: Recurring Period
recurprd.description: A gateway dependent value used to determine the interval between recurring payments.

dirgift.title: direct gift
dirgift.description: The designation of target or purpose the donor has specified for how they want their gift to be used.

shipenabled.description: Enable the collection of separate recipient details for the specific purpose of shipping.

shiptitle.title: shipping title
shiptitle.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Title to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Title**.

shipfname.title: shipping first name
shipfname.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for First Name to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **First Name**.

shiplname.title: shipping last name

shipemail.title: shipping email
shipemail.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Email to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Email Address**.

shipadd1.title: shipping address 1
shipadd1.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 1 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Address 1**.

shipadd2.title: shipping address 2
shipadd2.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 2 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field **Address 2**.

shipcity.title: shipping city
shipcity.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for City to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **City**.

shippostcode.title: shipping postcode
shippostcode.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Post or ZIP Code to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Postcode**.

shipregion.title: shipping region
shipregion.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Region to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Region**.

shipnotes.title: shipping notes
shipnotes.description: Shipping Notes. This may refer to any note added to shipping details, or any personal message added by the supporter to any electronic message or postal card that has been requested to be sent.

inmem.title: in memoriam
inmem.description:
Flag to indicate if the donation is being made in Memory or Honor of someone. 

 *In Memoriam* gifts allows the supporter to record additional details particularly relating to the memorial nature of the gift.  Note that **any other value** other than **Y** will result in the **In Memoriam** data **not** being recorded. All details supplied to the In Memoriam fields will be represented with a separate FIM transaction, which can be linked to the donation using the transaction identifiers.

honname.description: The name of the person in whose Honor the *In Memoriam Gift* is made.

infname.description: Name of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infemail.description: Email address of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infadd1.description: Address line 1 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infadd2.description: Address line 2 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift* - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

infcity.description: City of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infpostcd.description: Postcode of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infreg.description: Region, State/Province of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infcountry.description: Country of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

recnzegft.description: Indicates if the *In Memoriam Gift* should be specially recognised.

trbopts.description: Specific choices of tribute options for an *In Memoriam Gift*.

gftrsn.description: The reason the *In Memoriam Gift* is being made.
gftrsn.example: Tom was such a crucial member of our local community, and left such an impression on everyone he met.

get.description: List available supporter database fields that can be used with POST / PUT methods.

200.description:
An overview of the fields available for use with a supporter object when working in this account.

 The names of fields are account specific, and are determined at the time of field creation.  In order for the software to understand how and where the stored data is to be used, many fields are 'tagged' to a specific function.   These tags have a 'property', which can be used to refer to any supporter field in a standardised way when processing pages - regardless of the naming convention used in the account. 


The **standardFieldNames** parameter in page processing allows for referring to fields using values from the 'property' attribute in the field list.

schema.description: A list of fields available in the 'Account Data Structure'.

items.description: An overview of the attributes related to a field.

id.description: An automatically generated database identifier for this field.

name.description: The name given to this field by the user when it was added in the Account Data Structure.

tag.description:
The tag associated with this field. A tag identifies which functions within the application the field is linked to. The most important of these is the Email Address, which helps to determine how unique identifiers should be generated. 

 The Account Data Structure can also hold up to 300 'Not Tagged' fields. These are fields are generic in nature, and their purpose is determined by the user.

property.description:
Denotes a standardised property name that may be used to refer to this field in supporter objects when processing pages (see the parameter *standardFieldNames* in [**/page/{id}/process**](processPage))

 Fields which are listed as 'Not Tagged' will be named as NOT_TAGGED_1 to NOT_TAGGED_300, where the number will have been automatically assigned by the software when the fields were created as part of the 'Account Data Structure'.

schema.description: Describes parameters required to migrate a recurring payment from an external service.

campaignId.description:
A unique database identifier used to refer to a page, particularly in transactional data. 

 Where records in transactional data originate outside of a page structure, for example as a result of dashboard actions or imports, this will hold a value of 0, indicating that the transaction outcome should not be associated with a particular page.

oneOf.description: The token being migrated is for a credit or debit card payment schedule.

oneOf.description: The token being migrated is for a bank payment schedule.

paymentType.description: The payment type of the recurring payment to be migrated. Note that payment type is dependent on the processing gateway, and on whether the type is bank or credit card.

gatewayToken.description: A token supplied by the payment gateway - required to migrate this recurring schedule

currency.title: currency
currency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the currency used for this transaction. When processing payments, it is important to note that currency support is gateway dependent, so be sure to check with your designated payment gateway that the selected currency is supported and has been enabled for your account.

amount.description: The amount to be processed as part of this schedule. Must be a value greater than 0.

expiry.title: Recurring Expiry
expiry.description: The expiry date of the card associated with this recurring payment [MM/YYYY].

recurringFrequency.title: Recurring Frequency
recurringFrequency.description:
The schedule used for processing recurring payments. 

 Typical frequency for recurring charges are usually monthly, quarterly, every six months ('semi annually'), or annually.

recurringDay.title: Recurring Day
recurringDay.description: For recurring payment schedules, this denotes the day of the month when a payment should be processed.  If a Recurring Start Date was also provided, the processing day will be valid for the periods after the *initial* payment.

recurringStartDate.title: ISO formatted Recurring Start Date
recurringStartDate.description: The date when the *first* payment in a recurring payment schedule should be pushed [MM/DD/YYYY]. Works in conjunction with *Recurring Day*

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

To monitor the current Email suppression level, please refer to the *Email suppression* section under *Marketing Tools* in the dashboard. name.description: The name given to this question in the dashboard.

type.description: The type of the question represented.

oneOf.title: OPTIN
oneOf.description: An Opt-in type question. On pages, this type of question will usually be represented as a checkbox or radio button, and can only hold a value of 'Y' (representing and opted in status), or 'N' which represent *not* opted in, or *unsubscribed*.

oneOf.title: GENERAL
oneOf.description: A general type of question, which can typically be used to store any type of input - up to a character limit of 2000.

oneOf.title: CONFIRMATION
oneOf.description:
A type of Opt-in question which requires an additional step for the user to confirm their intention to Opt-in. The second step is in the form of a click on a link received via email. These types of questions can hold values of 'Y' (for fully Opted in supporter), 'P' (for supporters who are pending - they have not yet clicked the confirmation link), or 'N' (the supporter is opted out / or *unsubscribed*) <!-- theme: info --> 
> **Note that the links in confirmation emails sent out by the software will expire after 24 hours**

response.description:
The content recorded as a response for this entry.  The campaign type output is determined by the type of question submitted, combined with the specific content of the response. 'GENERAL' questions are least restrictive, and may contain up to 2000 characters of data. These types of questions are typically output with a campaign type of **QMR** in transactional data.

  **Note that the single character values Y, N, P and D are reserved**, and will generate **QCB** type transactions rather than **QMR** when output - regardless if question type configured in the account is set to be GENERAL.

oneOf.description: An Opt-in type question. On pages, this type of question will usually be represented as a checkbox or radio button, and can only hold a value of 'Y' (representing and opted in status), or 'N' which represent *not* opted in, or *unsubscribed*.

oneOf.title: GENERAL
oneOf.description: A general type of question, which can typically be used to store any type of input - up to a character limit of 2000.

oneOf.title: CONFIRMATION
oneOf.description:
A type of Opt-in question which requires an additional step for the user to confirm their intention to Opt-in. The second step is in the form of a click on a link received via email. These types of questions can hold values of 'Y' (for fully Opted in supporter), 'P' (for supporters who are pending - they have not yet clicked the confirmation link), or 'N' (the supporter is opted out / or *unsubscribed*) <!-- theme: info --> 
> **Note that the links in confirmation emails sent out by the software will expire after 24 hours**

response.description:
The content recorded as a response for this entry.  The campaign type output is determined by the type of question submitted, combined with the specific content of the response. 'GENERAL' questions are least restrictive, and may contain up to 2000 characters of data. These types of questions are typically output with a campaign type of **QMR** in transactional data.

  **Note that the single character values Y, N, P and D are reserved**, and will generate **QCB** type transactions rather than **QMR** when output - regardless if question type configured in the account is set to be GENERAL.

schema.description:
<!-- theme: info --> 
> #### A note on the naming of fields
> The given names for fields in an account will be dependent on how the data structure was set up. The list of items provided here uses standard tagged names for the fields. 
This call also provides access to the tagged field for 'Email Address', which allows for a change of the email address association of the unique identifier.

Email Address.title: supporter email
Email Address.description: The primary email address of a supporter, which will typically be used as the basis for identifying a unique supporter to which any transactions will be associated.

Title.title: supporter title
Title.description: A supporter's title to be used in salutations

Address 1.title: supporter address 1
Address 1.description: The first line of the supporter's address

Address 2.title: supporter address 2
Address 2.description: The second line of the supporter's address

Address 3.title: supporter address 3
Address 3.description: The third line of the supporter's address

Postcode.title: supporter postcode
Postcode.description: The supporter's postal or zip code

City.title: supporter city
City.description: The city in a supporter's address

Region.title: supporter region
Region.description: The supporter's region, state, county, province or similar

Appeal Code.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

Phone Number.title: supporter phonenumber
Phone Number.description: The primary phone number for a supporter

Anonymous Donor.description: Flag to indicate if a donation is being made anonymously. The flag is stored on the supporter record, but can be passed with individual donations if the field is included on a form.  **Anonymous donors names should not be publicised.**

Second Email Address.description:
Supporter's secondary email address. 

 **Note:** While this email field does **not** count as an indicator of the uniqueness of record, system settings may affect how this field behaves in particular with relation to external CRM systems.
  Such a setting may be enabled if your organization represents a 'Contact' with multiple email addresses. Any email address used on submission will be also checked against this field to identify a supporter. 
  If a match is found then a new supporter record will *not* be created, and the transaction will be associated with this supporter.

Second Address 2.description: Supporter's secondary address - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

Second Phone Number.description: Supporter's secondary phone number, e.g cell phone or mobile number. This field is used by the Messaging services to handle delivery via SMS.

Billing Address 2.description: Supporter's billing address - second line (floor, apartment, suite, etc. )

Supporter Organization.title: supporter organization
Supporter Organization.description: The name of the supporter's organization

Supporter Chapter.description: The chapter of an organization this supporter belongs to

Supporter Suffix.description: The suffix to use for the constituent

Supporter Birthday.description: Supporter's date of birth. The format YYYY-MM-DD is **required** for this field by the software.

Send Offset.description: Nearest Time Offset of the supporter's location.  When updating manually, the value **must** be in the correct format, and be one of the supported offset times (positive or negative) in hours and minutes from UTC.  Offsets that appear outside of the allowed values are shifted to the nearest available supported timezone.

questions.description: An object containing the questions we wish to record answers to for this supporter.  In the dashboard questions are organised as 'Questions' and 'Opt-in'. While both types are technically questions, their function is slightly different.  'Questions' are quite generic in nature, have a user defined role and can be used to collect (almost) any type of information. Questions created as an 'Opt-in' type have a specific role in the software, as they are used for recording supporter consent relating to any type of communication.

Opt-in.description:
An example question with the dashboard name 'Opt-in', set up in the dashboard to be an Opt-in.

 When the *standardFieldNames* parameter is used in /page/process, it is also possible to refer to questions by their id (as seen in /supporter/questions) using a name such as *question.{id}*'

Comments.description: An **example** question with the dashboard name 'Comments', set up in the dashboard to be a generic type of Question
Comments.example: I decided to opt-out of receiving your newsletters because I have too much stuff to read...

Change Email Address association.summary: Change the email address associated with this record.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of supporters matching a query. The currently available query types are:

  **latestCreated**: The latest supporter records created in the system. 

**latestModified**: The latest supporter records modified in the system. 
 
**suppressed**: Supporter records that are currently suppressed (excluded from receiving email) in the system. 
  
**search**: Locate supporters records that match filters on field values.

**profile**: Supporter records that belong to a particular profile.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of query to run. **Note**: A query for a 'profile' requires a 'profileId' to be provided. A 'search' query will require a 'filter' to be provided.

parameters.description: The name of an export group containing a list of supporter fields to be included. **Note**: Export groups can be managed in the dashboard.  For more information, please see our knowledgebase article on [export groups](https://knowledge.engagingnetworks.net/datareports/account-data-structure-settings#AccountDataStructureSettings-ManageField%E2%80%9CExportGroups%E2%80%9D).

schema.description: The name of an export group containing the list of supporter fields to be included. This setting is ignored if the export type is one of the "standard" types, as these will use a fixed set of fields and return them in a pre-defined order. **Note**: Export groups are managed from within the dashboard, and are useful for determining which constituency fields should be included data coming out of APIs.  For more information, please see our knowledgebase article on [export groups](https://knowledge.engagingnetworks.net/datareports/account-data-structure#AccountDataStructure-CreatinganExportGroup).

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The ID of a profile returning matching supporters. **Note**: Supporter profiles can be created and managed in the dashboard. For more details, read our knowledgebase article on [profiles](https://knowledge.engagingnetworks.net/datareports/supporter-profiles)

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
The constituent data filter to apply to a '**search**' type query. A query can be formulated as fieldName:value, where the following operators may be applied:

  The character **:** indicates *equals*. 

 The characters **||** indicate that a logical **OR** should be applied between values. 

  The character **~** indicates that an *additional* filter follows. Together the applied filters will be treated as an **AND** query.

 **As an example:** Applying a filter such as firstName:Bob~country:GB||US will match for supporters called Bob who are located in either Great Britain or the United States.

post.description:
Provides a facility to add supporters in bulk to an existing active marketing automation. 


Adding supporters using this method will skip the ‘query’ step for qualification, but any **exclusion filter** added in automation will still be adhered to. 


The method allows for a supporter to be added to an automation multiple times, but supporters can only join an automation once per 24 hour period. 
A summary of the identities of the supporters who were added by the call is provided as response to this call.


### Possible use cases ###
**NOTE:** *These particular use cases assumes that recurring payments are handled **outside** of Engaging Networks*, and the marketing tools are used for communication relating to the payment only.
* Notifying donors each time a recurring payment has been made. Some payment providers have specific requirements relating to how a merchant should handle communication with customers, including providing any receipt for payment which could have requirements for listing cancellation procedures for payment, receipt of email etc. 


* Notifying donors of an upcoming recurring charge when these charges are far apart (e.g annual or semi-annual subscriptions). This method could add supporters to a 'notification automation' a specified number of days ahead of the next charge.
Please consult with your payment providers for details on specific requirements for content.

supporterObjectQuestions.description: An array of question responses provided by a supporter

id.description: **questionId** is an alternative database identifier which is used to refer to this question in certain circumstances. The identifier must used when producing input for the **import.service** 'importTransactions' format. Use the [/supporter/questions](/operations/listSupporterQuestions) to look up the correct identifiers to use with the REST API and import.service.

name.description: The name given to this question in the dashboard.

type.description: The type of the question represented.

oneOf.title: OPTIN
oneOf.description: An Opt-in type question. On pages, this type of question will usually be represented as a checkbox or radio button, and can only hold a value of 'Y' (representing and opted in status), or 'N' which represent *not* opted in, or *unsubscribed*.

oneOf.title: GENERAL
oneOf.description: A general type of question, which can typically be used to store any type of input - up to a character limit of 2000.

oneOf.title: CONFIRMATION
oneOf.description:
A type of Opt-in question which requires an additional step for the user to confirm their intention to Opt-in. The second step is in the form of a click on a link received via email. These types of questions can hold values of 'Y' (for fully Opted in supporter), 'P' (for supporters who are pending - they have not yet clicked the confirmation link), or 'N' (the supporter is opted out / or *unsubscribed*) <!-- theme: info --> 
> **Note that the links in confirmation emails sent out by the software will expire after 24 hours**

response.description:
The content recorded as a response for this entry.  The campaign type output is determined by the type of question submitted, combined with the specific content of the response. 'GENERAL' questions are least restrictive, and may contain up to 2000 characters of data. These types of questions are typically output with a campaign type of **QMR** in transactional data.

  **Note that the single character values Y, N, P and D are reserved**, and will generate **QCB** type transactions rather than **QMR** when output - regardless if question type configured in the account is set to be GENERAL.

modifiedOn.description: Timestamp (UNIX epoch) of when this item was last modified

supporter.description:
Describes a supporter with the fields named using standardised field names. These can be utilised in [**/page/process**](pageProcess) to simplify code targetting supporter fields.

Note that the software allows for great flexibility when it comes to naming of fields, which means that the 'non-standard' names for each field will be dependent on the account.

emailAddress.title: supporter email
emailAddress.description: The primary email address of a supporter, which will typically be used as the basis for identifying a unique supporter to which any transactions will be associated.

title.title: supporter title
title.description: A supporter's title to be used in salutations

address1.title: supporter address 1
address1.description: The first line of the supporter's address

address2.title: supporter address 2
address2.description: The second line of the supporter's address

address3.title: supporter address 3
address3.description: The third line of the supporter's address

postcode.title: supporter postcode
postcode.description: The supporter's postal or zip code

city.title: supporter city
city.description: The city in a supporter's address

region.title: supporter region
region.description: The supporter's region, state, county, province or similar

phoneNumber.title: supporter phonenumber
phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number for a supporter

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

anonymousDonor.description: Flag to indicate if a donation is being made anonymously. The flag is stored on the supporter record, but can be passed with individual donations if the field is included on a form.  **Anonymous donors names should not be publicised.**

creditCardHolderName.title: card holder name
creditCardHolderName.description: The name of the credit card holder, as printed on the card. To be used when processing donations.

bankAccountNumber.title: bank account number
bankAccountNumber.description: The number of the bank account used for making donations.

bankRoutingNumber.title: routing number
bankRoutingNumber.description: A bank routing number - sometimes known as a 'sort code' - required for donations via bank.

bankAccountType.description: A gateway dependent value for the type of bank account to charge, e.g CHECKING or SAVINGS.

emailAddress2.description:
Supporter's secondary email address. 

 **Note:** While this email field does **not** count as an indicator of the uniqueness of record, system settings may affect how this field behaves in particular with relation to external CRM systems.
  Such a setting may be enabled if your organization represents a 'Contact' with multiple email addresses. Any email address used on submission will be also checked against this field to identify a supporter. 
  If a match is found then a new supporter record will *not* be created, and the transaction will be associated with this supporter.

secondAddress2.description: Supporter's secondary address - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

phoneNumber2.description: Supporter's secondary phone number, e.g cell phone or mobile number. This field is used by the Messaging services to handle delivery via SMS.

billingAddress2.description: Supporter's billing address - second line (floor, apartment, suite, etc. )

organization.title: supporter organization
organization.description: The name of the supporter's organization

chapter.description: The chapter of an organization this supporter belongs to

suffix.description: The suffix to use for the constituent

dateOfBirth.description: Supporter's date of birth. The format YYYY-MM-DD is **required** for this field by the software.

geo.description: The supporter's geographical location, as indicated by coordinates supplied as latitude and longitude.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location, North or South (-) of the equator.

longitude.description: The longitude of the location, East or West (-) of the Greenwhich meridian.

sendOffset.description: Nearest Time Offset of the supporter's location.  When updating manually, the value **must** be in the correct format, and be one of the supported offset times (positive or negative) in hours and minutes from UTC.  Offsets that appear outside of the allowed values are shifted to the nearest available supported timezone.

NOT_TAGGED_1.description:
Up to 300 generic fields can be created to be used to store any type of information. 

 These fields are automatically assigned the next available number (1-300) when they are added as part of an *Account Data Structure*, so their functional assignment in a particular account should be determined by a call to **/supporter/fields**, or by a quick visit to the dashboard where both the field name and the assigned 'tag' can be observed.

 **DO NOT** push any data into numbered fields that have not yet been assigned a function in the account's *Data Structure*.

questions.description: An object containing the questions we wish to record answers to for this supporter.  In the dashboard questions are organised as 'Questions' and 'Opt-in'. While both types are technically questions, their function is slightly different.  'Questions' are quite generic in nature, have a user defined role and can be used to collect (almost) any type of information. Questions created as an 'Opt-in' type have a specific role in the software, as they are used for recording supporter consent relating to any type of communication.

Opt-in.description:
An example question with the dashboard name 'Opt-in', set up in the dashboard to be an Opt-in.

 When the *standardFieldNames* parameter is used in /page/process, it is also possible to refer to questions by their id (as seen in /supporter/questions) using a name such as *question.{id}*'

Comments.description: An **example** question with the dashboard name 'Comments', set up in the dashboard to be a generic type of Question
Comments.example: I decided to opt-out of receiving your newsletters because I have too much stuff to read...

transaction.description: Provides the necessary parameters required to set up a netdonor payment process.

donationAmt.description: Processing amount, presented as a decimal number without any currency symbols.  The amount may be omitted for pagetypes where the amount to be processed is calculated by the page processing, for example when purchasing tickets for events or certain types of symbolic gifts. The amount will also include any additions made by any optional Fee Cover requested.

paymenttype.title: payment type
paymenttype.description: Passed as a value or determined by a payment gateway, this describes the payment type to use for the transaction. Valid types may vary depending on the payment gateway used.	  Some payment types, e.g **CASH**, **CHECK** or **WIRE** may be used to indicate offline donations, or to process 'free' items. Such donations will not be possible to reconcile with an external payment gateway.	 In output, the prefix **TEST:** indicates a transaction carried out in a *DEMO* mode. These transactions should also not be considered for reconciliation purposes. 	 The prefix **digitalwallet:** is added if the payment was made using a digital wallet integration.

paycurrency.title: currency
paycurrency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the currency used for this transaction. When processing payments, it is important to note that currency support is gateway dependent, so be sure to check with your designated payment gateway that the selected currency is supported and has been enabled for your account.

ccnumber.title: credit card number
ccnumber.description: The credit card number to charge when processing this payment. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

ccexpire.title: card expiry
ccexpire.description: The card expiry date. The exact format is dependent on the payment gateway used to process the transaction.

ccvv.description: A verification value or code printed on the credit card being used for this transaction. May be 3 or 4 characters depending on the card issuer. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

recurrpay.title: Recurring Payment
recurrpay.description: This donation should be recurring according to a schedule.

recurrfreq.title: Recurring Frequency
recurrfreq.description:
The schedule used for processing recurring payments. 

 Typical frequency for recurring charges are usually monthly, quarterly, every six months ('semi annually'), or annually.

recurrday.title: Recurring Day
recurrday.description: For recurring payment schedules, this denotes the day of the month when a payment should be processed.  If a Recurring Start Date was also provided, the processing day will be valid for the periods after the *initial* payment.

recurrstart.title: ISO formatted Recurring Start Date
recurrstart.description: The date when the *first* payment in a recurring payment schedule should be pushed [MM/DD/YYYY]. Works in conjunction with *Recurring Day*

recurrend.title: Recurring Expiry
recurrend.description: The expiry date of the card associated with this recurring payment [MM/YYYY].

recurrcnt.title: Recurring Count
recurrcnt.description: The number of recurring donation payments to process with this schedule.

recurprd.title: Recurring Period
recurprd.description: A gateway dependent value used to determine the interval between recurring payments.

dirgift.title: direct gift
dirgift.description: The designation of target or purpose the donor has specified for how they want their gift to be used.

shipenabled.description: Enable the collection of separate recipient details for the specific purpose of shipping.

shiptitle.title: shipping title
shiptitle.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Title to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Title**.

shipfname.title: shipping first name
shipfname.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for First Name to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **First Name**.

shiplname.title: shipping last name
shiplname.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Last Name to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Last Name**.

shipemail.title: shipping email
shipemail.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Email to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Email Address**.

shipadd1.title: shipping address 1
shipadd1.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 1 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Address 1**.

shipadd2.title: shipping address 2
shipadd2.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 2 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field **Address 2**.

shipcity.title: shipping city
shipcity.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for City to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **City**.

shippostcode.title: shipping postcode
shippostcode.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Post or ZIP Code to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Postcode**.

shipregion.title: shipping region
shipregion.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Region to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Region**.

shipnotes.title: shipping notes
shipnotes.description: Shipping Notes. This may refer to any note added to shipping details, or any personal message added by the supporter to any electronic message or postal card that has been requested to be sent.

inmem.title: in memoriam
inmem.description:
Flag to indicate if the donation is being made in Memory or Honor of someone. 

 *In Memoriam* gifts allows the supporter to record additional details particularly relating to the memorial nature of the gift.  Note that **any other value** other than **Y** will result in the **In Memoriam** data **not** being recorded. All details supplied to the In Memoriam fields will be represented with a separate FIM transaction, which can be linked to the donation using the transaction identifiers.

honname.description: The name of the person in whose Honor the *In Memoriam Gift* is made.

infname.description: Name of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infemail.description: Email address of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infadd1.description: Address line 1 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infadd2.description: Address line 2 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift* - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

infcity.description: City of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infpostcd.description: Postcode of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infreg.description: Region, State/Province of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

infcountry.description: Country of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

recnzegft.description: Indicates if the *In Memoriam Gift* should be specially recognised.

trbopts.description: Specific choices of tribute options for an *In Memoriam Gift*.

gftrsn.description: The reason the *In Memoriam Gift* is being made.
gftrsn.example: Tom was such a crucial member of our local community, and left such an impression on everyone he met.

othamt1.title: other1
othamt1.description: **Other 1**: A client defined tagged field which can be used to store any additional data that may relate to the current transaction.
othamt1.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

othamt2.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

othamt3.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

othamt4.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

example.othamt3: I can also provide sandwiches for the event

product.description: Applicable only to donation pages with the sub-type PREMIUM. Selects the product chosen by the donor. Note that if values for both product selectors are provided, the *productVariantId* will take precedence.

productVariantId.description: The unique database identifier for a product variant.

productSku.description: The user assigned Stock Keeping Unit for this product variant.

recurringTransactionObject.description: Describes parameters required to migrate a recurring payment from an external service.

campaignId.description:
A unique database identifier used to refer to a page, particularly in transactional data. 

 Where records in transactional data originate outside of a page structure, for example as a result of dashboard actions or imports, this will hold a value of 0, indicating that the transaction outcome should not be associated with a particular page.

type.description: The type of recurring transaction schedule being migrated into this system.
type.title: Method of Payment

oneOf.title: CREDIT_RECURRING
oneOf.description: The token being migrated is for a credit or debit card payment schedule.

oneOf.title: BANK_RECURRING
oneOf.description: The token being migrated is for a bank payment schedule.

paymentType.title: payment type
paymentType.description: The payment type of the recurring payment to be migrated. Note that payment type is dependent on the processing gateway, and on whether the type is bank or credit card.

gatewayToken.description: A token supplied by the payment gateway - required to migrate this recurring schedule

currency.title: currency
currency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the currency used for this transaction. When processing payments, it is important to note that currency support is gateway dependent, so be sure to check with your designated payment gateway that the selected currency is supported and has been enabled for your account.

amount.description: The amount to be processed as part of this schedule. Must be a value greater than 0.

expiry.title: Recurring Expiry
expiry.description: The expiry date of the card associated with this recurring payment [MM/YYYY].

recurringFrequency.title: Recurring Frequency
recurringFrequency.description:
The schedule used for processing recurring payments. 

 Typical frequency for recurring charges are usually monthly, quarterly, every six months ('semi annually'), or annually.

recurringDay.title: Recurring Day
recurringDay.description: For recurring payment schedules, this denotes the day of the month when a payment should be processed.  If a Recurring Start Date was also provided, the processing day will be valid for the periods after the *initial* payment.

recurringStartDate.title: ISO formatted Recurring Start Date
recurringStartDate.description: The date when the *first* payment in a recurring payment schedule should be pushed [MM/DD/YYYY]. Works in conjunction with *Recurring Day*

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

questionsObject.description: An object containing the questions we wish to record answers to for this supporter.  In the dashboard questions are organised as 'Questions' and 'Opt-in'. While both types are technically questions, their function is slightly different.  'Questions' are quite generic in nature, have a user defined role and can be used to collect (almost) any type of information. Questions created as an 'Opt-in' type have a specific role in the software, as they are used for recording supporter consent relating to any type of communication.

An example question with the dashboard name 'Opt-in', set up in the dashboard to be an Opt-in.

demoMode.description: Request the page process run in 'demo' mode, which for donation processing means using a 'test' gateway with appropriate testing credentials rather than real cards, and for advocacy actions such as email to target all email traffic generated will go to the participating supporter's email address.

suppressAutoResponder.description: Do not send an autoresponder ('thank you email') after the page submission is completed.

standardFieldNames.description: Use standardised rather than account specific names within the 'supporter' object when referring to fields.

trackingId.description: A value which is usually used to denote the origin of incoming traffic. May be passed as a the parameter as part of a link to a page using: **ea.tracking.id**

appealCode.description:
An appeal code assigned to this submission. 

 The value may be passed as a field present on the page, or with the url parameter **supporter.appealCode**. Any appeal code will be recorded on the supporter's record, and in regular page processing it will also be noted on the transactional level. 

!!! note "Using appealCode with ENS"
 	When posting data using [processPage](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processPage) or [processCardPayment](https://developer.engagingnetworks.net/api/rest/index.html#/operations/processCardPayment), the function of this field is more flexible, and can be *either* part of the supporter object **or** of the page object, and will be stored separately in each. The page-builder behaviour can easily be replicated by passing identical values for appealCode to **both** the page object **and** the supporter object in the post request.

Where both parameters are present in the URL, precedence can be controlled by page level settings. **Note**: Content will be automatically truncated if the maximum length is exceeded, and the last three characters replaced by '...' to indicate that a string has been truncated.
