supporter_title.title: supporter title
supporter_title.description: A supporter's title to be used in salutations

address1.title: supporter address 1
address1.description: The first line of the supporter's address

address2.title: supporter address 2
address2.description: The second line of the supporter's address

address3.title: supporter address 3
address3.description: The third line of the supporter's address

postcode.title: supporter postcode
postcode.description: The supporter's postal or zip code

city.title: supporter city
city.description: The city in a supporter's address

region.title: supporter region
region.description: The supporter's region, state, county, province or similar

phoneNumber.title: supporter phonenumber
phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number for a supporter

anonymousDonor.description: Flag to indicate if a donation is being made anonymously. The flag is stored on the supporter record, but can be passed with individual donations if the field is included on a form.  **Anonymous donors names should not be publicised.**

creditCardHolderName.title: card holder name
creditCardHolderName.description: The name of the credit card holder, as printed on the card. To be used when processing donations.

bankAccountNumber.title: bank account number
bankAccountNumber.description: The number of the bank account used for making donations.

bankRoutingNumber.title: routing number
bankRoutingNumber.description: A bank routing number - sometimes known as a 'sort code' - required for donations via bank.

bankAccountType.description: A gateway dependent value for the type of bank account to charge, e.g CHECKING or SAVINGS.

secondEmail.description:
Supporter's secondary email address. 

 **Note:** While this email field does **not** count as an indicator of the uniqueness of record, system settings may affect how this field behaves in particular with relation to external CRM systems.
  Such a setting may be enabled if your organization represents a 'Contact' with multiple email addresses. Any email address used on submission will be also checked against this field to identify a supporter. 
  If a match is found then a new supporter record will *not* be created, and the transaction will be associated with this supporter.

secondAddress2.description: Supporter's secondary address - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

secondPhoneNumber.description: Supporter's secondary phone number, e.g cell phone or mobile number. This field is used by the Messaging services to handle delivery via SMS.

billingAddress2.description: Supporter's billing address - second line (floor, apartment, suite, etc. )

organization.title: supporter organization
organization.description: The name of the supporter's organization

chapter.description: The chapter of an organization this supporter belongs to

suffix.description: The suffix to use for the constituent

dateOfBirth.description: Supporter's date of birth. The format YYYY-MM-DD is **required** for this field by the software.

geo.description: The supporter's geographical location, as indicated by coordinates supplied as latitude and longitude.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location, North or South (-) of the equator.

longitude.description: The longitude of the location, East or West (-) of the Greenwhich meridian.

sendOffset.description: Nearest Time Offset of the supporter's location.  When updating manually, the value **must** be in the correct format, and be one of the supported offset times (positive or negative) in hours and minutes from UTC.  Offsets that appear outside of the allowed values are shifted to the nearest available supported timezone.

NOT_TAGGED.description:
Up to 300 generic fields can be created to be used to store any type of information. 

 These fields are automatically assigned the next available number (1-300) when they are added as part of an *Account Data Structure*, so their functional assignment in a particular account should be determined by a call to **/supporter/fields**, or by a quick visit to the dashboard where both the field name and the assigned 'tag' can be observed.

 **DO NOT** push any data into numbered fields that have not yet been assigned a function in the account's *Data Structure*.

amount.description: Processing amount, presented as a decimal number without any currency symbols.  The amount may be omitted for pagetypes where the amount to be processed is calculated by the page processing, for example when purchasing tickets for events or certain types of symbolic gifts. The amount will also include any additions made by any optional Fee Cover requested.

paymentType.title: payment type
paymentType.description: Passed as a value or determined by a payment gateway, this describes the payment type to use for the transaction. Valid types may vary depending on the payment gateway used.	  Some payment types, e.g **CASH**, **CHECK** or **WIRE** may be used to indicate offline donations, or to process 'free' items. Such donations will not be possible to reconcile with an external payment gateway.	 In output, the prefix **TEST:** indicates a transaction carried out in a *DEMO* mode. These transactions should also not be considered for reconciliation purposes. 	 The prefix **digitalwallet:** is added if the payment was made using a digital wallet integration.

currency.title: currency
currency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the currency used for this transaction. When processing payments, it is important to note that currency support is gateway dependent, so be sure to check with your designated payment gateway that the selected currency is supported and has been enabled for your account.

creditCardNumber.title: credit card number
creditCardNumber.description: The credit card number to charge when processing this payment. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

creditCardExpiry.title: card expiry
creditCardExpiry.description: The card expiry date. The exact format is dependent on the payment gateway used to process the transaction.

creditCardVerificationValue.description: A verification value or code printed on the credit card being used for this transaction. May be 3 or 4 characters depending on the card issuer. **Note:** We do not process any credit card information, so this value will be represented by a token.

recurringPayment.title: Recurring Payment
recurringPayment.description: This donation should be recurring according to a schedule.

recurringFrequency.title: Recurring Frequency
recurringFrequency.description:
The schedule used for processing recurring payments. 

 Typical frequency for recurring charges are usually monthly, quarterly, every six months ('semi annually'), or annually.

recurringDay.title: Recurring Day
recurringDay.description: For recurring payment schedules, this denotes the day of the month when a payment should be processed.  If a Recurring Start Date was also provided, the processing day will be valid for the periods after the *initial* payment.

recurringStartDateISO.title: ISO formatted Recurring Start Date
recurringStartDateISO.description: The date when the *first* payment in a recurring payment schedule should be pushed [MM/DD/YYYY]. Works in conjunction with *Recurring Day*

recurringExpiry.title: Recurring Expiry
recurringExpiry.description: The expiry date of the card associated with this recurring payment [MM/YYYY].

recurringCount.title: Recurring Count
recurringCount.description: The number of recurring donation payments to process with this schedule.

recurringPeriod.title: Recurring Period
recurringPeriod.description: A gateway dependent value used to determine the interval between recurring payments.

directGift.title: direct gift
directGift.description: The designation of target or purpose the donor has specified for how they want their gift to be used.

enabled.description: Enable the collection of separate recipient details for the specific purpose of shipping.

shipping_title.title: shipping title
shipping_title.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Title to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Title**.

shipping_firstName.title: shipping first name
shipping_firstName.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for First Name to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **First Name**.

shipping_lastName.title: shipping last name
shipping_lastName.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Last Name to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Last Name**.

shipping_email.title: shipping email
shipping_email.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Email to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Email Address**.

shipping_address1.title: shipping address 1
shipping_address1.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 1 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Address 1**.

shipping_address2.title: shipping address 2
shipping_address2.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Address 2 to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field **Address 2**.

shipping_city.title: shipping city
shipping_city.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for City to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **City**.

shipping_postcode.title: shipping postcode
shipping_postcode.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Post or ZIP Code to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Postcode**.

shipping_region.title: shipping region
shipping_region.description: Applicable if Shipping Enabled is set to 'Y' on submission, and describes the entry for Region to use for shipping purposes. If shipping is not enabled for this transaction, then this value will typically reflect the value on the supporter's record for the tagged field for **Region**.

notes.title: shipping notes
notes.description: Shipping Notes. This may refer to any note added to shipping details, or any personal message added by the supporter to any electronic message or postal card that has been requested to be sent.

inMemoriam.title: in memoriam
inMemoriam.description:
Flag to indicate if the donation is being made in Memory or Honor of someone. 

 *In Memoriam* gifts allows the supporter to record additional details particularly relating to the memorial nature of the gift.  Note that **any other value** other than **Y** will result in the **In Memoriam** data **not** being recorded. All details supplied to the In Memoriam fields will be represented with a separate FIM transaction, which can be linked to the donation using the transaction identifiers.

honoreeName.description: The name of the person in whose Honor the *In Memoriam Gift* is made.

informName.description: Name of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informEmail.description: Email address of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informAddress1.description: Address line 1 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informAddress2.description: Address line 2 of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift* - floor, apartment, suite, etc.

informCity.description: City of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informPostcode.description: Postcode of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informRegion.description: Region, State/Province of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

informCountry.description: Country of the person being informed of an *In Memoriam Gift*.

recognizeGift.description: Indicates if the *In Memoriam Gift* should be specially recognised.

tributeOptions.description: Specific choices of tribute options for an *In Memoriam Gift*.

giftReason.description: The reason the *In Memoriam Gift* is being made.
giftReason.example: Tom was such a crucial member of our local community, and left such an impression on everyone he met.

other1.title: other1
other1.description: **Other 1**: A client defined tagged field which can be used to store any additional data that may relate to the current transaction.
other1.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

other2.title: other2
other2.description: **Other 2**: A client defined tagged field which can be used to store any additional data that may relate to the current transaction.
other2.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

other3.title: other3
other3.description: **Other 3**: A client defined tagged field which can be used to store any additional data that may relate to the current transaction.
other3.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

other4.example: Text containing some information that could prove useful when we handle this transaction, for example additional notes from a supporter directly relating to this particular donation.

productvariant_id.description: The unique database identifier for a product variant.

sku.description: The user assigned Stock Keeping Unit for this product variant.

additionalAmount.description: An (optional) additional donation amount, which is part of the calculated total for the ticket order.

discount.description: The discount code applied to the ticket order.

ticket_id.description: A numerical identifier representing a ticket.

quantity.description: The number of tickets in this order. **Note**: When passing this as input, the associated details for an equal number of attendees should also be submitted.

attendee_address1.description: An attendee's first line of address.

attendee_address2.description: An attendee's second line of address.

dietaryPreference.description: Notes on an attendee's dietary preferences, if any.

mobilityRequirement.description: Notes on an attendee's mobility requirements, if any.

custom1.description: **Custom 1**: A client defined field used to store additional data. The content of this field will be automatically truncated if maximum length is exceeded, with the three last characters replaced by ... to indicate that the string has been truncated

custom2.description: **Custom 2**: A client defined field used to store additional data. The content of this field will be automatically truncated if maximum length is exceeded, with the three last characters replaced by ... to indicate that the string has been truncated

promoCode.description: A promotional code to apply to this order.

variantId.description: An identifier representing the particular variant of a membership. The variants created in the dashboard indicate different membership terms, each with separate duration and cost.

member_address1.description: An associated member's first line of address

member_postcode.description: An associated member's ZIP or postcode

transactional_id.description: The database identifier used to refer to a campaign

txType.title: transaction type
txType.description: The type of fundraising transaction being described.

oneOf.title: CASH
oneOf.description: The transaction was recorded as a cash payment.

oneOf.title: CHECK
oneOf.description: The transaction was recorded as a payment made via check.

oneOf.title: CREDIT_SINGLE
oneOf.description: The transaction is for a one-off payment taken using a credit or debit card.

oneOf.title: CREDIT_RECURRING
oneOf.description: The transaction is part of a recurring payment schedule using a credit or debit card.

oneOf.title: RECUR_UNMANAGED
oneOf.description: The transaction is part of a recurring payment schedule, but the processing of associated payments is not carried out by Engaging Networks.

oneOf.title: BANK_RECURRING
oneOf.description: The transaction is part of a recurring payment schedule taking funds directly from a donors bank account.

oneOf.title: BANK_SINGLE
oneOf.description: The transaction is for a one-off payment taken directly from a donors bank account.

transactionId.title: gateway transaction id
transactionId.description: The transaction identifier as provided by a payment gateway. If the current transaction is a refund, then this identifier points back to the transaction being refunded. The specific formatting of the identifier will be dependent on the gateway.

transactionError.description: Any message, including error codes provided by a payment gateway if a transaction is rejected. Refer to the documentation provided by the relevant payment gateway for details.
transactionError.example: The card type provided is not supported for this transaction

folder_id.description: A numerical identifier for a folder. The default value of 0 denotes the 'Home' folder in the dashboard.

component_id.description: The database identifier assigned to this component.

component_name.description: The name this item can be found under in the dashboard

ownedBy.description: The id of the user who is holds the ownership of this item

profileId.description: The numerical identifier of a supporter Profile

content.description: The content described by this textblock, including any HTML

senderEmailId.description: Identifies the email sender object used as a sender for this thank you email.

replyToEmailId.description: Identifies the reply to object for the response recipient (if supported).

receiptType.description: The type of content to send. Sending the message in both HTML and plain text is supported.

contentText.description: The content of the message in plain text format
contentText.example: Thank you for your signature, {user_data~First Name}. Together we can make a difference!

contentHtml.description: The content of the message in HTML format
contentHtml.example:
<html> <head> <title>Thank you!</title> </head> <body data-gramm="false"> Thank you for your signature, {user_data~First Name}. 
Together we can make a difference! </body> </html>

widgetType.description: The type of widget represented by this item

description.description: A note by an author describing the function of the code contained within this block.

header.description: HTML used to form the header of a wrapper template. Output **above** any page output.

footer.description: HTML used to form the footer of a wrapper template. Output **below** any page output.

mobileHeader.description: If present, a dedicated mobile version of this header will be output if a browser identifies as a mobile device.

mobileFooter.description: If present, a dedicated mobile version of this footer will be output if a browser identifies as a mobile device.

field_id.description: An automatically generated database identifier for this field.

field_name.description: The name given to this field by the user when it was added in the Account Data Structure.

tag.description:
The tag associated with this field. A tag identifies which functions within the application the field is linked to. The most important of these is the Email Address, which helps to determine how unique identifiers should be generated. 

 The Account Data Structure can also hold up to 300 'Not Tagged' fields. These are fields are generic in nature, and their purpose is determined by the user.

property.description:
Denotes a standardised property name that may be used to refer to this field in supporter objects when processing pages (see the parameter *standardFieldNames* in [**/page/{id}/process**](processPage))

 Fields which are listed as 'Not Tagged' will be named as NOT_TAGGED_1 to NOT_TAGGED_300, where the number will have been automatically assigned by the software when the fields were created as part of the 'Account Data Structure'.

importformat_id.description: The database identifier used to refer to this import format.

importformat_name.description: The reference name given to this import format.

fields.description: An array containing the headings which should be associated with fields, pages, questions and other items which may have been mapped within this format.

**[DO_NOT_MAP]** Used to indicate any field you *do not* want to map - e.g if a data header exists in a file, but you do not want it included in import.


**household_id** – Used for householding, the parent supporter id (Household Parent)


**household_relationship** – Relationship to parent.


**household_salutation** – A salutation used when addressing a household.


**hpc_value** – highest previous contribution value.


**hpc_currency** – higest provious contribution currency.


**originsource_category** – Origin source category.


**originsource_code** – Origin source code.


**geo_latitude** – Geomapping latitude.


**geo_longitude** – Geomapping longitude.


**ContactId** – Salesforce Contact ID.

question_id.description: The database identifier for this question.

questionId.description: **questionId** is an alternative database identifier which is used to refer to this question in certain circumstances. The identifier must used when producing input for the **import.service** 'importTransactions' format. Use the [/supporter/questions](/operations/listSupporterQuestions) to look up the correct identifiers to use with the REST API and import.service.

question_name.description: The name given to this question in the dashboard.

type_short.description: The type of the question represented.

oneOf.title: OPT
oneOf.description: An Opt-in type question. On pages, this type of question will usually be represented as a checkbox or radio button, and can only hold a value of 'Y' (representing and opted in status), or 'N' which represent *not* opted in, or *unsubscribed*.

oneOf.title: GEN
oneOf.description: A general type of question, which can typically be used to store any type of input - up to a character limit of 2000.

oneOf.title: CONF
oneOf.description:
A type of Opt-in question which requires an additional step for the user to confirm their intention to Opt-in.  The second step is in the form of a click on a link received via email.  These types of questions can hold values of 'Y' (for fully Opted in supporter), 'P' (for supporters who are pending - they have not yet clicked the confirmation link), or 'N' (the supporter is opted out / or *unsubscribed*) <!-- theme: info --> 
> **Note that the links in confirmation emails sent out by the software will expire after 24 hours**

label.description: The label to be displayed for this question when used on pages.

htmlFieldType.description: The HTML type specified to be used to represent this item on-page

transaction_status.title: status
transaction_status.description: The status of this transaction, as reported by the designated payment gateway.

oneOf.title: success
oneOf.description: The transaction was successfully processed by the payment gateway.

oneOf.title: reject
oneOf.description: The transaction was rejected by the payment gateway.

oneOf.title: refund
oneOf.description: The transaction has been marked as refunded.

oneOf.title: change
oneOf.description: A change was made to the transaction.

reject.summary: The transaction was rejected by the payment gateway

datestampEpoch.description: The date when this transaction or item was generated [Unix epoch]

suppressed.description:
Flag to indicate whether the supporter is excluded from being sent any messages by the software. 

 The flag is updated automatically by the software and is based on email delivery feedback such as bounces, which may indicate that the address is invalid.  To monitor the current Email suppression level, please refer to the *Email suppression* section under *Marketing Tools* in the dashboard. The suppression status of individual supporters can be refreshed using the dashboard's *Manage Supporter* widget.

type_long.description: The type of the question represented.

oneOf.title: OPTIN
oneOf.description: An Opt-in type question. On pages, this type of question will usually be represented as a checkbox or radio button, and can only hold a value of 'Y' (representing and opted in status), or 'N' which represent *not* opted in, or *unsubscribed*.

oneOf.title: GENERAL
oneOf.description: A general type of question, which can typically be used to store any type of input - up to a character limit of 2000.

oneOf.title: CONFIRMATION
oneOf.description:
A type of Opt-in question which requires an additional step for the user to confirm their intention to Opt-in. The second step is in the form of a click on a link received via email. These types of questions can hold values of 'Y' (for fully Opted in supporter), 'P' (for supporters who are pending - they have not yet clicked the confirmation link), or 'N' (the supporter is opted out / or *unsubscribed*) <!-- theme: info --> 
> **Note that the links in confirmation emails sent out by the software will expire after 24 hours**

response.description:
The content recorded as a response for this entry.  The campaign type output is determined by the type of question submitted, combined with the specific content of the response. 'GENERAL' questions are least restrictive, and may contain up to 2000 characters of data. These types of questions are typically output with a campaign type of **QMR** in transactional data.

  **Note that the single character values Y, N, P and D are reserved**, and will generate **QCB** type transactions rather than **QMR** when output - regardless if question type configured in the account is set to be GENERAL.
